title: appintents: expose PID and TTY on TerminalEntity
body:
This is a re-submission of #10983. Vouched in #11287.

Add child process PID and TTY device name as queryable properties on
TerminalEntity for App Intents. This enables external applications and
Shortcuts to identify and filter terminals by their PID or TTY device.

Closes #10756

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: ocean6954

linked_issues.title: macOS: Expose TTY, PID on TerminalEntity within App Shortcuts
appintents: expose PID and TTY on TerminalEntity

issue_comments.body: Hi @ocean6954, just checking in — are you still working on this? I have a use case for this feature (switching to Claude Code sessions by PID in a terminal switcher app). Let me know if there's anything I can help with!

Hi @grimmerk

Sorry for the late reply! I had already pushed the fixes but wasn't sure how to re-request a review — I just did that now. Thanks for your interest in this feature!
@ocean6954 Did you want to rebase this on top of the core changes from #11639 ?
Rebased on top of #11639 and refactored to use getProcessInfo(). Squashed into a single commit.
This has been superseded by #11922 right?

review_comments.body: These should live on the Ghostty.Surface model instead of here, its been a long transition to move over there.
We should use native Zig optionals here, not sentinel integral values.
Store as a zig type, not this.

What do you think about returning a `pid_t`? That is the POSIX type for an integer representing a process ID.

Only using 32 bits is restrictive, while `pid_t` is kind of opaque in that each platform can define it differently. You never know if Ghostty will exist on platforms supporting larger that 32 bit process IDs. I would assume that there are equivalent types in Swift and Zig.
The core API returns a `u64`. I chose that specifically over a `pid_t` to avoid any sort of platform dependence (Zig seems to be going hard against POSIX in the upcoming 0.16 release).

@tristan957 

I've rebased on top of #11639 and refactored to use the getProcessInfo() API. As @jcollie mentioned, the core API returns u64. This PR converts it to i64 at the C API boundary to allow returning -1 when unavailable.
`0` _should_ work as a signal that the PID was unavailable, at least on any system I'm aware of that's not a valid PID.
Good point, updated to use u64 with 0 as the sentinel value.

label_notes: External-contributor PR superseded after substantial collaborative iteration. ocean6954 (prior_prs=1, NONE) submitted +43/-0 across 5 files adding PID and TTY queryable properties on macOS TerminalEntity for App Intents (re-submission of #10983, vouched in #11287, closes #10756). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 disclosure in body. Open ~6 weeks with substantive multi-reviewer engagement: mitchellh (CONTRIBUTOR) requested architectural changes ('These should live on the Ghostty.Surface model instead', 'use native Zig optionals here, not sentinel integral values'); tristan957 (MEMBER) proposed pid_t type; jcollie (MEMBER) explained design rationale ('chose u64 specifically over pid_t to avoid platform dependence — Zig seems to be going hard against POSIX in 0.16'); jcollie requested rebase on top of #11639 core changes; jcollie corrected sentinel-value approach ('0 should work as a signal that the PID was unavailable'). ocean6954 addressed each: rebased on #11639, refactored to getProcessInfo() API, switched i64→u64 with 0-sentinel. Real user interest signaled (grimmerk and dnwpark both requested for Claude Code session switcher use cases). 00-kat (CONTRIBUTOR) ultimately identified supersession: 'This has been superseded by #11922 right?' — closed without further comment. Closure-reason: superseded by successor PR after design-iteration collaboration. Code quality genuinely good through review iterations. Different from earlier superseded cases (godot#116422 wrong-abstraction-layer, poetry#10741 pure-workflow): here superseded after extensive successful collaboration where original work informed final implementation. Adds: AI-disclosed-external-contributor-superseded-after-collaborative-iteration sub-pattern. Diversity: ghostty rejected_quality (first), non-mvanhorn/non-hakonhagland ghostty case.
